name: The Vierville Bluff Crest
description: The top of the hundred-foot chalk bluff above the Vierville Draw, where the Atlantic Wall's inland face is exposed and the beach below is visible in its full, terrible geometry — the first high ground the Allies have taken on Dog Green.
atmosphericDetails: From here, the entire Dog Green Sector is visible below — the surf line, the open sand, the shingle, the wire — reduced to a diagram of the last ninety minutes.
The second wave of LCAs is visible on the horizon, their wakes white against the grey Channel, moving toward whatever the draw has become.
The wind at the crest is steady and cold and carries the smell of the beach upward — a smell that will not leave the men who are standing here for the rest of their lives.
